Vyapar alternative

A Vyapar alternative for businesses that need more than fast billing.

Vyapar is widely used for billing and basic business records. Fastbooks focuses on the next layer: turning bills, receipts, bank transactions, and voice notes into review-ready accounting entries for owners and accountants.

Get Early Access on WhatsApp

We respect your privacy. Message opens directly in WhatsApp; no info is saved on this site.

See WhatsApp accounting

Direct answer

Is Fastbooks a Vyapar alternative?

Fastbooks focuses on a different pain than Vyapar. While Vyapar is tailored for offline-first retail shop billing and inventory, Fastbooks targets the daily capture-and-review pipeline. Fastbooks converts receipts, bank transactions, bills, and voice notes into structured drafts, keeping the accountant at the centre of the approval process.

  • Capture inputs from WhatsApp and mobile easily.
  • Focuses on accounting preparation instead of just inventory billing.
  • Ensures final control remains under accountant review.

The Review Angle

Billing-first vs accounting-review-first

Billing software helps create invoices quickly. But daily accounts work does not end at billing. Businesses still need purchase entries, expense records, bank receipts, payments, narration, party matching, GST review, and accountant approval. Fastbooks is designed around this accounting-review workflow. It does not only ask, “Can we make a bill quickly?” It asks, “Can we prepare the accounting entry from whatever proof the business has?”

Workflow comparison

Vyapar-style billing workflow vs Fastbooks accounting-preparation workflow

Aspect Vyapar-style billing workflow Fastbooks preparation workflow
Main use case Billing, invoices, basic inventory, business records. Accounting entry preparation from bills, receipts, bank rows, and voice notes.
Starting point User opens app and creates bill or record. User sends transaction proof or context, then reviews the prepared draft.
Inventory and billing Strong fit for shop billing and inventory-led use cases. Useful when the bigger pain is accountant-ready sales, purchase, expense, receipt, and payment preparation.
Accountant workflow Business owner may manage records directly. Designed so accountant/CA review remains central.
Bank entries Bank/payment data may still need manual interpretation. Bank rows can be turned into receipt/payment drafts with review status.
Best fit Retailers who primarily want billing and inventory. Businesses that want daily accounting context captured and prepared for review.

When to choose

When Fastbooks is a better fit than a billing-first app

Your accountant asks for bills later

Fastbooks helps capture bills and receipts before they are forgotten.

Bank entries take too long

Prepare receipt and payment drafts from transaction text or bank rows.

Staff are not trained accountants

Staff can send context. The accountant can review accounting treatment later.

You do not want blind automation

Fastbooks keeps uncertain entries in review instead of posting them without approval.

Honest fit

Fastbooks may not be the right fit if:

You need simple billing or ERP

  • You only need simple invoice creation.
  • You want a mature full-suite accounting system today.

You want hands-off automation

  • You want a fully automatic posting system with no review.
  • Your current accounting team does not want a review-first workflow.

FAQ

Vyapar alternative questions

Is Fastbooks better than Vyapar?

Not universally. Vyapar-style tools are useful for billing and inventory-heavy shop workflows. Fastbooks is better positioned for businesses that want accounting entry preparation and accountant review from many daily inputs.

Can Fastbooks do billing?

Fastbooks is being built around GST billing and accounting workflows. The stronger differentiation is that it also prepares accounting entries from bills, receipts, bank rows, and voice notes.

Is Fastbooks good for retailers?

Yes, especially retailers who want sales, purchases, expenses, receipts, payments, bank entries, and GST hints prepared for accountant review. Avoid positioning it only as a POS/inventory tool.

Should I switch from Vyapar immediately?

No. During early access, first map your workflow: billing, purchases, bank receipts, expense capture, inventory needs, and accountant handoff. Fastbooks should be adopted where it reduces manual entry and improves review quality.
